source.title: BSEE Accident Investigation Report - Structural Damage, Holstein Spar Rig

actions_taken: West knuckle boom crane was taken out of service
Pipe gripper was removed from service and sent to third-party crane service company for analysis
Third-party hydraulic service company performed in-depth analysis of hydraulic circuit
Anadarko plans to upgrade knuckle boom crane with visual and audible indicators for proper gripping force
Additional training planned for all Crane Operators on proper crane operation procedures
event.summary: On February 3, 2021, at 02:00 hours, a Nabors Offshore night Crane Operator operating the West knuckle boom crane on the Holstein Spar Rig was transferring a single joint of 5.5-inch drill pipe (31.5 feet long, 810 pounds) from the pipe conveyor to the pipe rack. The Crane Operator gripped the pipe after only 2 seconds instead of the manufacturer-required 5 seconds, and the gripper feet were not in full contact with the pipe before gripping. When the operator abruptly stopped the boom swing, the pipe gripper shook and bounced erratically, causing the drill pipe to slip out and fall approximately 13 feet onto the pipe rack. The pipe then bounced and slid over the handrail into the Gulf of Mexico. No injuries were reported. Investigation determined the cause was operator error: failure to follow manufacturer's operating instructions regarding grip time and proper gripper foot contact.
recommendations: Upgrade knuckle boom crane with visual and audible indicators to confirm proper gripping force to Crane Operator
Provide additional training to all Crane Operators with the upgrades
Ensure proper crane operation procedures are followed according to manufacturer's recommendations

controls.name: Job Safety Analysis (JSA) Review
controls.barrier_role: Administrative control to ensure crew awareness of hazards and procedures

supporting_text: At 18:00 hours a tour change took place and a Job Safety Analysis (JSA) on 'Picking Up Pipe with Knuckle Boom Crane' was reviewed and signed by all participating drill crew members
Crane Operator was unaware he should hold the gripper closed for 5 seconds as per manufacture's recommendation

controls.name: Pre-Operating Inspection of Crane
controls.barrier_role: Engineering/maintenance control to verify equipment condition

supporting_text: The night time Crane Operator performed a pre-operating inspection of the West knuckle boom crane with no issues noted
There were no issues noted in the service report for the crane or pipe grippers after the annual inspection

controls.name: Pre-Operating Checklist Review
controls.barrier_role: Administrative control to verify equipment readiness before operation

supporting_text: A Pre-Operating Checklist was reviewed and signed by the Task Supervisor and Crane Operator after coming on tour at 18:00 hours, with no deficiencies noted

controls.name: Manufacturer's Operating Instructions (5-Second Grip Time)
controls.barrier_role: Engineering control specifying proper equipment operation procedure

supporting_text: The manufacturer states to confirm that the fixed gripper feet are in contact with the pipe before attempting to grip, and the pipe must be gripped for 5 seconds before attempting the lift
It only took the crane operator approximately 2 seconds to grip the pipe before he started the lift

The Crane Operator failed to follow the manufacture's operating instructions per OEM document which states: 'Lower grab onto pipe until guide pieces rest on the pipe. Close grab and hold control in Close Position for approximately five seconds to ensure that the pipe is gripped firmly.'

controls.name: Gripper Feet Contact Verification
controls.barrier_role: Engineering control to ensure proper load engagement

supporting_text: Video footage reveals that the Crane Operator lowered the pipe gripper and gripped the pipe before the fixed gripper feet were in contact with the pipe
The manufacturer states to confirm that the fixed gripper feet be in contact with the pipe before attempting to grip

controls.name: Smooth Boom Motion Control
controls.barrier_role: Operational control to prevent load instability

supporting_text: The Crane Operator abruptly stopped swinging the boom and the pipe gripper began to bounce and shake erratically
Video footage reveals that the Crane Operator stopped the boom swing motion abruptly causing the pipe gripper to shake and bounce uncontrollably

controls.name: Area Clearance (No Personnel in Load Path)
controls.barrier_role: Administrative control to prevent personnel injury from falling load

supporting_text: No one was in the area at the time of the incident
No injuries were reported related to this incident

controls.name: Safety Shutdown Procedure
controls.barrier_role: Administrative control to halt operations and notify personnel after incident

supporting_text: A Safety Shutdown was called immediately and proper personnel were notified

notes.rules: JSON output only. mentioned fields must be evidence-based. Use null for unknown values.
